experiments.purpose: Probe the capability-lift pattern's recipe, super-protocol unification options (A refinement, B parameterized Carrier<Underlying>, C both), API broadening at four levels, and limits (generic Underlying, ~Copyable Underlying, existentials). Six variants V0–V5.
experiments.statusDetail: V0 Cardinal baseline, V1 Ordinal baseline with refinement, V2 super-protocol via refinement, V3 super-protocol via SE-0346 parameterization, V4 API broadening at four levels, V5 limits (three sub-cases catalogued). Moved from swift-institute/Experiments/ on 2026-04-24 when swift-carrier-primitives was created.

experiments.purpose: Empirically test whether Span<Element>: Carrier is viable in the Q3 (Copyable × ~Escapable) quadrant, contra Research/sli-span-family.md DECISION (2026-04-24). Four variants: V1 default extension, V2 explicit witnesses same-module, V3 cross-module use, V4 release-mode validation per [EXP-017].
experiments.statusDetail: V1 REFUTED (default `Carrier where Underlying == Self` extension is gated to Self: Escapable; ~Escapable Self witness candidates are excluded entirely). V2/V3/V4 CONFIRMED with explicit witnesses (`@_lifetime(borrow self) _read { yield self }` + `@_lifetime(copy underlying) init(_:)`). Cross-module generic algorithms returning `C.Underlying` as a function result require `where C.Underlying: Copyable`. The production default extension does NOT cover Span; per-Span explicit bodies are required (~5 lines each). Refines sli-span-family.md concern #1 with a sharper failure mode and confirms concern #3 (explicit bodies, not one-liners).

experiments.purpose: Test whether sibling default extensions on Carrier (each with its own constraint suppression) can cover the ~Escapable, ~Copyable, and ~Copyable & ~Escapable trivial-self quadrants — collapsing per-conformer explicit witnesses to one-liners.
experiments.statusDetail: V1 (Q3 ~Escapable Self) CONFIRMED — Span: Carrier with one-line typealias works through the Q3 default. V2 (Q2 ~Copyable Self) CONFIRMED — synthetic UniqueValue conforms with one-line typealias through Q2 default. V3 (Q4 ~Copyable & ~Escapable Self) CONFIRMED — synthetic ScopedValue conforms with one-line typealias through Q4 default. Three sibling default extensions cover Q2/Q3/Q4; the existing Q1 default remains. @_lifetime annotations are in the ~Escapable variants (Q3, Q4) where the result type is ~Escapable; they remain absent from Q1 and Q2 (Escapable Self rejects them). Closes the Open item in sli-span-family.md v1.1.0.
crossRefs: Sources/Carrier Primitives/Carrier where Underlying == Self, Self ~Escapable.swift
Sources/Carrier Primitives/Carrier where Underlying == Self, Self ~Copyable.swift
Sources/Carrier Primitives/Carrier where Underlying == Self, Self ~Copyable & ~Escapable.swift

experiments.purpose: Empirically determine whether @dynamicMemberLookup applied to the Carrier super-protocol (with a default KeyPath subscript on a protocol extension) is viable across all four Copyable × Escapable quadrants of Underlying.
experiments.statusDetail: V1 (Q1 Copyable & Escapable) CONFIRMED — protocol-level @dynamicMemberLookup propagates to conformers; default subscript on protocol extension instantiates correctly. V2 (Q2 ~Copyable) REFUTED — protocol-extension subscript carries implicit `where Self: Copyable, Self.Underlying: Copyable`. V3 (Q3 ~Escapable) REFUTED — implicit `where Self: Escapable, Self.Underlying: Escapable`. Q4 REFUTED by transitivity. Verdict: dynamic member lookup at the Carrier protocol level is a Q1-only affordance. Decision (do not add to Carrier; consumers may adopt locally on their own Q1 conformers) is captured in Research/Carrier Primitives Vision.md §'Read-only by design (no Mutable / no @dynamicMemberLookup / no cross-Carrier utilities at v0.1.x)'.

narrativeSections.heading: Provenance
narrativeSections.body: This experiment was authored in swift-institute/Experiments/ on 2026-04-22 and moved to this package on 2026-04-24. See Research/Carrier Primitives Vision.md §'Capability-lift pattern' for variant verdicts (the original Research/capability-lift-pattern.md was consolidated into the Vision document on 2026-04-29).
